name: Separator
purpose: Creates low-noise visual separation between related sections, rows, or panel regions.
whenToUse: Use to separate dense content groups inside cards, sheets, or menus.
Use a vertical separator only when two adjacent regions benefit from a visual divider.
whenNotToUse: Do not stack separators where spacing alone already provides enough structure.
Do not use separators as decorative flourishes unrelated to hierarchy.
accessibilityRequirements: Treat separators as decorative by default so assistive technologies do not announce meaningless structure.
If a separator conveys meaningful grouping, pair it with nearby headings or labels instead of relying on the line alone.
